shell.errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
shell.welcome.tagline: An operating system for your mind.
shell.welcome.nameTaken: A vault named “{name}” already exists. Choose a different name.
shell.welcome.cloudWarning.title: Heads up — this looks like a {service} folder.
shell.welcome.cloudWarning.hint: You can still continue, but Brainstorm’s own sync (when it ships) will be a better fit. To silence this warning, pick a different location.
shell.welcome.vaultHint: A vault is a self-contained directory holding all your data. You can move or back it up by copying the folder.
shell.welcome.starterContentHint: A few example notes and a project to show you around. You can edit or remove them anytime.
shell.welcome.starterContentTemplateHint: Replaced by the template you picked below. You can edit or remove everything anytime.
shell.welcome.templates.heading: Or start from a template
shell.welcome.templates.hint: Pick a ready-made vault to explore. It’s added under its own collection, so you can remove the whole thing later.
shell.welcome.templates.importFailedBody: Your vault was created, but the template couldn’t be added. You can add starter content later from Settings.
shell.welcome.startStepSubtitle: Add a few example notes, pick a use-case template, or skip and start with an empty vault.
shell.welcome.createFailed: Couldn’t create the vault. Please try again.
shell.welcome.openFailed: Couldn’t open the vault. Please try again.
shell.dashboard.vaultInfo.label: Vault info
shell.dashboard.vaultInfo.title: Vault info
shell.dashboard.vaultInfo.backendInsecur: Credentials backend is insecure — secrets are not encrypted on disk.
shell.dashboard.vaultSwitcher.title: Switch vault
shell.settings.title: Settings
shell.settings.backupMigration.summary: Export your whole vault to a portable file, or import data from a JSON / JSONL file onto a vault type.
shell.settings.backupMigration.export.hi: Write the entire vault — entities, rich-text bodies, links, and attachments — to a single .bsbundle file for backup or moving to another device.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.hi: Import rows from a JSON or JSONL file. A non-destructive preview shows what would be created or updated before anything is written.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.pi: Could not read the selected file.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ta: The vault entity type the rows map onto, e.g. brainstorm/Note/v1.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.ty: Enter a target entity type first.
shell.settings.backupMigration.import.pl: Could not build the import preview.
Will create {create} and update {update} of {total} records.
Choose an Obsidian vault folder. Each note's frontmatter becomes properties, [[wikilinks]] and ![[embeds]] become links, #tags are kept, and referenced attachments are imported as files. Re-importing the same vault updates rather than duplicating.
Could not read the selected folder.
The vault entity type each note becomes, e.g. brainstorm/Note/v1.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.hi: Choose a Notion "Markdown & CSV" export (.zip). Pages become entities with their properties and body, databases become rows, the page tree becomes parent links, and internal links are preserved. Re-importing the same export updates rather than duplicating.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.pi: Could not read the selected export.
shell.settings.backupMigration.notion.ta: The vault entity type each page becomes, e.g. brainstorm/Note/v1.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.summary: Deleted objects from every app are kept restorable for the retention window, then removed for good. Restore or permanently delete them from the Recently Deleted overlay.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.retention: Items older than this are permanently removed the next time Recently Deleted is opened.
shell.settings.recentlyDeleted.items.cou: {count, plural, =0 {Nothing waiting in Recently Deleted} one {# item can be restored} other {# items can be restored}}
shell.settings.ai.title: AI providers
shell.settings.ai.intro: Bring your own API key for a cloud model. Keys are stored encrypted in this vault and never leave your device except to call the provider you configure. The built-in local model needs no key.
shell.settings.ai.anthropic.name: Anthropic (Claude)
shell.settings.ai.anthropic.hint: Used when an app or workflow requests the Anthropic provider. Get a key from console.anthropic.com.
shell.settings.ai.openai.name: OpenAI
shell.settings.ai.openai.hint: OpenAI and OpenAI-compatible endpoints (OpenRouter, Together, Groq, local servers). Get a key from platform.openai.com.
shell.settings.ai.glm.name: z.ai (GLM)
shell.settings.ai.glm.hint: z.ai's GLM models (OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions). Get a key from z.ai.
shell.settings.ai.mistral.name: Mistral AI
shell.settings.ai.mistral.hint: Mistral's European models (OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions). Get a key from console.mistral.ai.
shell.settings.ai.gemini.name: Google Gemini
shell.settings.ai.gemini.hint: Google's Gemini models. Get a key from aistudio.google.com.
shell.settings.ai.replacePlaceholder: Paste a new key to replace
shell.settings.ai.usageHint: AI calls recorded on this device, by app. Counts and tokens only — never your prompts or the responses.
shell.settings.ai.routingHint: Which model handles a request when an app or workflow doesn't pin one. Apps can still ask for a specific provider.
shell.settings.ai.budgetHint: Set a monthly token ceiling per app. Budgets are recorded now and shown against usage; automatic cut-off arrives with billing.
shell.settings.ai.budgetCurrent: {count, plural, one {# token budget} other {# token budget}}
shell.settings.mcp.title: MCP servers
shell.settings.mcp.intro: Connect external Model Context Protocol servers so the Agent can use their tools. Each server's tools are offered only to conversations you grant it. Tool descriptions come from the server and are untrusted — review them before enabling.
shell.settings.mcp.spawnConsentNote: This runs a program on your computer. An agent can only launch it after you grant the default-off “run local servers” permission — review the exact command above first.
shell.settings.mcp.authReplacePlaceholde: Enter a new token to replace the stored one
shell.settings.mcp.inspectFailed: Could not reach this server. It will show as down and its tools won't be offered.
shell.settings.mcp.untrustedNote: Tool names and descriptions below are provided by the server and are untrusted. Review them before granting this server to a conversation.
shell.settings.interface.controls.title: Header controls
shell.settings.interface.controls.summar: Choose which controls appear in the dashboard header.
shell.settings.interface.control.setting: Always visible — it's how you reach this screen.
shell.settings.interface.clock.title: Clock
shell.settings.region.language.title: Language
shell.settings.region.language.summary: The language used across the interface. Changes apply immediately.
shell.settings.region.language.label: Display language
shell.settings.region.formats.title: Regional formats
shell.settings.region.formats.summary: Override how dates, times and numbers are formatted. Leave on Automatic to follow your system.
shell.settings.region.hourCycle.label: Time format
shell.settings.region.dateStyle.label: Date format
shell.settings.region.firstDay.label: First day of week
shell.settings.region.number.label: Number format
shell.settings.region.timezone.label: Time zone
shell.settings.notifications.osNative.de: Also show alerts in your operating system's notification center.
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.title: Do not disturb
shell.settings.notifications.dnd.summary: Mute popups during these hours. Notifications still appear in the notification center.
shell.settings.notifications.perApp.summ: Turn notifications off for individual apps.
shell.settings.notifications.center.coun: {count, plural, =0 {No notifications} one {# notification} other {# notifications}} · {unread} unread
shell.notifications.center.title: Notifications
shell.settings.membership.title: Power up your second brain.
shell.settings.membership.subtitle: One workspace for every thought, file, and connection. Stay free forever, or unlock the deep features that turn Brainstorm into a daily driver.
shell.settings.membership.cycle.label: Billing cycle
shell.settings.membership.tier.free.name: Free
shell.settings.membership.tier.free.tagl: Everything you need to get started.
shell.settings.membership.tier.pro.name: Pro
shell.settings.membership.tier.pro.tagli: For knowledge workers who live in their notes.
shell.settings.membership.tier.team.name: Team
shell.settings.membership.tier.team.tagl: Built for groups that think together.
shell.settings.membership.feature.free.4: Single device sync via local folder
shell.settings.membership.feature.team.2: Shared team vaults with role-based access
shell.settings.membership.trust.encrypte: Your vault is sealed on your device. We never see the keys.
shell.settings.membership.trust.local.bo: Your data lives in a folder you own. Cancel anytime, keep everything.
shell.settings.membership.trust.cancel.b: No contracts. Downgrade or stop your plan in one click.
shell.settings.membership.faq.title: Common questions
shell.settings.membership.faq.q1: What happens to my data if I downgrade?
shell.settings.membership.faq.a1: Nothing. Your vault is a folder on your disk. Pro-only features simply switch off — every note, file, and block stays where it is, fully readable.
shell.settings.membership.faq.a2: Yes. Yearly billing saves you 20% compared with the monthly plan, and you can switch between cycles whenever you want.
shell.settings.membership.faq.a3: You're charged per active seat, prorated to the day. Add or remove teammates anytime — no minimums, no commitments.
shell.settings.membership.faq.q4: Do you offer education or non-profit pricing?
shell.settings.membership.faq.a4: Students, teachers, and registered non-profits get Pro free, forever. Reach out from this screen once your vault is set up.
shell.settings.membership.checkout.place: Brainstorm is in preview — billing wires up later this stage. We saved your selection so we can take you straight to checkout when it ships.
shell.whatsNew.popover.title: What's new in Brainstorm
shell.openExplainer.inVaultOpeners.title: Opened in {app}
shell.openExplainer.storedDefault.title: Opened in {app} (your default)
shell.openExplainer.osHandoff.title: Opened with the system default
shell.openExplainer.handled.title: Opened in {app}
shell.openExplainer.refused.dangerousSch: That kind of link can run code, so Brainstorm won't open it.
shell.openExplainer.refused.noHandler.ti: Nothing in this vault can open that
shell.openExplainer.refused.noHandler.bo: Install an app that handles this kind, or allow it to open with the system default in Settings → Defaults.
shell.openExplainer.refused.unknownTarge: Brainstorm couldn't tell what kind of target this is.
shell.osHandoff.prompt.title: Open outside the vault?
shell.osHandoff.prompt.intro: Brainstorm doesn't have an in-vault opener for {signature} links. Open it with your system default?
shell.osHandoff.prompt.reason: Allow will remember this choice for all {signature}-style targets. Cancel just dismisses this one prompt.
shell.openWith.prompt.title: Open with…
shell.openWith.prompt.intro: More than one app can open {signature} targets. Which one should handle this?
shell.lock.title: Vault locked
shell.lock.subtitle: Enter your PIN to unlock.
shell.lock.cooldown: Too many attempts — wait {seconds, plural, one {# second} other {# seconds}}.
shell.lock.capped: Too many incorrect attempts. To continue, close the vault and reopen it with your passphrase.
shell.help.title: Help
shell.help.search.label: Search help
shell.cheatsheet.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shell.settings.keyboard.hint: Customize keyboard shortcuts for the shell. Press a row to record a new chord, or reset it to the default. Bindings persist with your vault and travel between macOS and Windows.
shell.settings.keyboard.capturePrompt: Press the keys for your shortcut…
shell.settings.keyboard.captureKeepWaiti: Add a non-modifier key to finish the chord.
shell.settings.keyboard.bareModifier: Add a key after the modifier
shell.settings.contributions.summary: Installed apps can add actions to other apps' menus — like Summarize or Share to. Turn an app's contributions off to remove them from every menu. This never affects the app itself.
shell.settings.contributions.contributes: Adds actions to other apps' menus
shell.settings.contributions.toggle: Allow {app} to add actions to other apps' menus
shell.settings.defaults.summary: Choose which app opens each kind of object, URL scheme, or file extension. Anything left on Automatic uses the app that registered for that target, falling back to Notes for objects and the operating system for schemes and files.
shell.settings.defaults.empty: No object types, schemes, or extensions are registered in this vault yet.
shell.settings.search.summary: Global search uses a local full-text index over every entity in this vault. It rebuilds itself from your data on each launch and after edits — these controls are for when you want to check or refresh it by hand.
shell.settings.search.unavailable: Open a vault to see its search index.
shell.settings.search.byType.heading: By type
shell.settings.search.reindexConfirm.bod: Rebuilds the search index from scratch. Safe to run anytime — search stays available throughout.
shell.settings.data.summary: Properties and dictionaries are vault-level: every app (Notes, Database, Graph, future) sees the same catalog. Add the shapes once; reuse them everywhere.
shell.settings.data.properties.heading: Properties
shell.settings.data.properties.dictionar: Create a dictionary first to back a Select / Multi-select property.
A new dictionary with the same name will be created automatically. You can rename or merge it later from the Dictionaries tab.
shell.settings.data.properties.deleteCon: Delete property “{name}”? Existing values stay on the notes that use it.
Currently used by {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}} — those values will become orphan rows.
shell.settings.data.properties.usagePill: Used by {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}}
shell.settings.data.properties.itemUsage: {count, plural, one {# object uses this value} other {# objects use this value}}
shell.settings.data.properties.allowedTy: Choose which object types this property can link to. Leave none selected to link to anything.
No object types are registered yet — this property will link to anything.
shell.settings.data.properties.kindLocke: Kind can't change after a property is created — existing values would be unreadable in the new shape.
shell.settings.data.dictionaries.heading: Dictionaries
shell.settings.data.dictionaries.deleteC: Delete dictionary “{name}”? Properties that reference it will be unusable until you reassign them.
shell.settings.themes.summary: Switch between the built-in themes. Your choice is per-vault and applies instantly across the shell.
shell.settings.themes.defaultDark.label: Default Dark
shell.settings.themes.defaultDark.descri: The shipped dark surface — warm violet accent on neutral grey glass.
shell.settings.themes.defaultLight.label: Default Light
shell.settings.themes.defaultLight.descr: Light counterpart of Default Dark — bright white glass with the same accent identity.
shell.settings.themes.midnight.label: Midnight
shell.settings.themes.midnight.descripti: Deep navy surfaces with a cool sky-blue accent. Pairs well with starry wallpapers.
shell.settings.themes.sepia.label: Sepia
shell.settings.themes.sepia.description: Warm paper-toned surfaces and a serif body stack. Tuned for long-form reading.
shell.settings.themes.highContrast.label: High Contrast
shell.settings.themes.highContrast.descr: Pure black on white with a saturated yellow accent. Maximises legibility.
shell.settings.themes.solar.label: Solar
shell.settings.themes.solar.description: Crisp cool-white surfaces with a warm orange accent. Bright and energetic.
shell.settings.themes.forest.label: Forest
shell.settings.themes.forest.description: Deep evergreen surfaces with a clear emerald accent. Calm woodland dark.
shell.settings.themes.nord.label: Nord
shell.settings.themes.nord.description: Arctic blue-grey slate surfaces with a soft frost-blue accent. Cool and easy on the eyes.
shell.settings.themes.aurora.label: Aurora
shell.settings.themes.aurora.description: Deep violet surfaces lit by a vivid fuchsia accent, like northern lights at night.
shell.settings.themes.mint.label: Mint
shell.settings.themes.mint.description: Fresh, faintly green-tinted white surfaces with a confident teal accent.
shell.settings.themes.rose.label: Rose
shell.settings.themes.rose.description: Soft blush-toned surfaces with a warm rose accent. Inviting and gentle.
shell.settings.themes.slate.label: Slate
shell.settings.themes.slate.description: Cool neutral-grey surfaces with a crisp indigo accent. Businesslike and clean.
shell.settings.appearance.summary: Pair a theme and wallpaper for Light and Dark. Pick how the active pair is chosen below — Auto follows your system.
shell.settings.appearance.mode.label: Appearance mode
shell.settings.general.placeholder: General settings will fill in as features come online. See docs/shell/25-settings.md.
shell.settings.updates.title: Updates
shell.settings.updates.summary: Check for a newer version of Brainstorm, then download and install it.
shell.settings.updates.channel.descripti: Beta receives new versions earlier; they may be less stable.
shell.settings.updates.unknown: Couldn't check for updates. Check your connection and try again.
shell.settings.security.appLock.title: App lock
shell.settings.security.appLock.descript: Require a PIN to unlock this vault after it locks.
shell.settings.security.appLock.removeCo: This vault will no longer require a PIN to unlock.
shell.settings.security.appLock.autoLock: After {count, plural, one {# minute} other {# minutes}} idle
shell.settings.security.insecureSuffix: — credentials are NOT encrypted on disk
shell.iconPicker.dropHint: Drop an image here or click to choose
shell.iconPicker.libraryEmpty: No uploaded icons yet. Upload one from the Upload tab.
shell.marketplace.title: Marketplace
shell.marketplace.kindFilter.label: Filter by kind
shell.marketplace.kind.app.label: App
shell.marketplace.kind.app.description: Runs code in a sandboxed renderer.
shell.marketplace.kind.theme.label: Theme
shell.marketplace.kind.theme.description: Token set, icon pack, and typography.
shell.marketplace.empty: Nothing in {panel} matches the current filter. Adjust the kind chip or search term to widen the view.
shell.marketplace.updates.label: Available updates

shell.marketplace.updates.consent.body: This update requests new permissions: {caps}. Update only if you trust this app.
shell.marketplace.detail.theme.activated: {name} is now the active theme. Every open window re-paints.
shell.marketplace.detail.app.uninstallCo: This removes {name} and revokes its capabilities. Any open windows for this app will close. The app's data is preserved on disk for now.
shell.marketplace.sources.summary: Catalogs the marketplace pulls from. The built-in source can't be removed; remote catalogs will be subscribable in a future iteration.
shell.marketplace.sources.addComingSoon: Subscribing to remote catalogs lands when the catalog protocol ships.
shell.bin.title: Bin
shell.bin.subtitle: Deleted objects are kept here until you restore or permanently delete them.
shell.bin.emptyHint: Objects you delete from any app land here first — nothing is lost until you empty the Bin.
shell.bin.purgeConfirm.title: Permanently delete {title}?
shell.bin.purgeConfirm.body: This can't be undone. {title} and its links are removed for good.
shell.bin.emptyConfirm.title: Empty the Bin?
shell.bin.emptyConfirm.body: This permanently deletes all {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}} in the Bin. This can't be undone.
shell.bin.restoredToast.title: Restored
shell.bin.purgedToast.title: Permanently deleted
shell.bin.emptiedToast.title: Bin emptied
shell.bin.emptiedToast.body: Removed {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}} for good.
shell.bin.purgeManyConfirm.title: Permanently delete {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}}?
shell.bin.purgeManyConfirm.body: This can't be undone. The selected {count, plural, one {object and its links are} other {objects and their links are}} removed for good.
shell.bin.restoredManyToast.title: Restored
shell.bin.restoredManyToast.body: Put {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}} back.
shell.bin.purgedManyToast.title: Permanently deleted
shell.bin.purgedManyToast.body: Removed {count, plural, one {# object} other {# objects}} for good.

shell.dashboard.dev.reseedToast.body: Release plan seeded — {created} new entities, {healed} healed, {resynced} re-synced, {removed} removed, {links} links.

shell.settings.spellcheck.languagesAuto: Spelling languages are detected automatically by your operating system.

shell.settings.files.hoursAgo: {count, plural, one {# hour ago} other {# hours ago}}

shell.settings.files.revokeConfirm.body: {appId} will lose access to {filename} immediately. The app must re-pick the file to regain access.
shell.settings.identity.displayNameHint: The name collaborators see for you across shared channels, comments, and member lists. It's a convenience label — your cryptographic identity stays your key.
shell.settings.identity.fingerprintHint: Your sovereign public-key fingerprint. Compare it out-of-band with a collaborator to confirm you're talking to the right person.
shell.settings.devices.title: Devices
shell.settings.devices.summary: Devices paired with this vault sync end-to-end encrypted updates over the configured relay.
shell.settings.devices.emptySubtitle: Add a device to sync your vault, or join an existing one.
shell.settings.devices.modeSasComingSoon: Coming soon — use QR code for now.
shell.settings.devices.noRelay: Pairing requires a configured sync relay on this vault. Add one before pairing a device.

shell.settings.devices.add.qrAlt: Pairing payload as a QR code
shell.settings.devices.add.handshakeInst: Pairing in progress — confirm the codes match on both screens.
shell.settings.devices.add.expired: Pairing expired. Start over to add this device.
shell.settings.devices.join.title: Join an existing vault
shell.settings.devices.join.scanInstruct: Point your camera at the pairing QR shown on the other device.
shell.settings.devices.join.pasteInstruc: Paste the pairing payload from the other device's Add-device screen.
shell.settings.devices.join.pasteInvalid: Payload doesn't look like a valid pairing code.
shell.settings.devices.join.confirmInstr: These codes should match — confirm if they do.
shell.settings.devices.join.pasteOnlyNot: Your platform doesn't support camera QR scanning; paste the payload instead.
shell.settings.devices.join.cameraDenied: Camera access was declined. Paste the payload below to continue.
shell.settings.devices.join.expired: Pairing expired. Start over to join this vault.
shell.settings.devices.revokeConfirm.bod: {deviceLabel} will no longer be able to read new updates from this vault.

shell.welcome.migrate.subtitle: Bring your notes, databases, files and links in as native objects — types, properties and links preserved.
shell.welcome.migrate.files: Markdown, HTML, CSV, JSON or JSONL files
shell.welcome.migrate.hint: First create a vault to import into — the import wizard opens automatically in Settings → Backup & Migration.
shell.welcome.migrate.start: Create a vault to import into
shell.dashboard.syncStatus.tooltip.local: This vault has no sync relay configured. Pair a device in Settings to sync.
shell.dashboard.syncStatus.tooltip.synci: Connected — recent traffic in the last 5 seconds.
shell.dashboard.syncStatus.tooltip.stale: Connected, but no traffic for over 30 seconds. Updates are still flowing if they arrive.
shell.dashboard.syncStatus.tooltip.error: The relay reported an error. Will retry automatically.
shell.dashboard.syncStatus.popover.title: Sync status
shell.settings.sync.summary: Live status for the sync relay configured on this vault. Pair devices in the Devices section above.
shell.settings.sync.unavailable: Open a vault to see sync status.
shell.settings.sync.policy.summary: Choose which shared items this device keeps in sync. Items left out aren't downloaded here until you open them; your data is never deleted.
shell.settings.sync.policy.mode.label: Sync
shell.settings.sync.restore.title: Restore from sync
shell.settings.sync.restore.summary: This device is empty but signed in. Recover your synced items from the sync service — they're decrypted here with your device key.
shell.settings.sync.restore.done: Restored {restored} of {requested, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}.
shell.settings.sync.restore.failed: Restore couldn't complete. Check your connection and try again.
shell.permissions.camera.title: Allow camera for QR scanning?
shell.permissions.camera.body: Brainstorm needs camera access for one-time QR scanning. You can paste the payload instead.
shell.settings.network.summary: Audit what's leaving the vault, control the proxy, and tune link previews.
shell.settings.network.proxy.title: Active proxy
shell.settings.network.proxy.useOverride: Override the shell-wide proxy for this vault
shell.settings.network.privacy.title: Link previews + embeds
shell.settings.network.privacy.strictHin: This vault is in a privacy-strict location, so previews default to Off.
shell.settings.network.privacy.allowlist: No hosts on the allowlist yet — previews will be refused.
shell.settings.network.automation.title: Automation network access
shell.settings.network.automation.warnin: Hosts your Automations may send requests to. An automation granted a host can transmit vault data there — only add hosts you trust. Each host is approved individually; there is no “any host” option.
shell.settings.network.automation.empty: No hosts approved — Automations HTTP steps are blocked until you add one.
shell.settings.network.automation.revoke: Automations will no longer be able to send requests to this host.
shell.settings.network.perApp.title: Per-app egress
shell.settings.network.perApp.summary: Apps that contacted the network in the last 7 days.
shell.settings.network.perApp.empty: No apps have contacted the network in this vault.
shell.settings.network.perApp.revokeConf: This app will stop being able to fetch, preview, or embed any URL.
shell.settings.network.recent.title: Recent requests
shell.settings.network.recent.empty: No requests in the last 24 hours.
shell.settings.network.blocked.title: Blocked requests
shell.settings.network.blocked.empty: No blocked requests in the last 24 hours.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.su: Sites you allowed or blocked from using your camera, microphone, or location in the in-app browser. Everything else is denied by default.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.permissions.em: No site permissions yet — the browser asks before any site can use a device.
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.title: Browser egress by host
shell.settings.webPrivacy.egress.summary: Hosts contacted by pages loaded in the in-app browser. Counts only — addresses and page contents are never recorded.
shell.settings.network.cache.title: Preview cache
shell.settings.network.cache.clearConfir: Cached link previews will be discarded. Future previews will be re-fetched.
shell.settings.network.embeds.title: Embed providers
shell.settings.network.embeds.placeholde: Curated provider list ships with the Stage 9 embed sandbox.
shell.settings.network.feedback.title: Send feedback
shell.settings.network.feedback.summary: Help us improve Brainstorm by sending anonymous bug reports, ideas and questions. The product works without ever phoning home — feedback is opt-in.
shell.settings.network.feedback.toggle: Send anonymized feedback when I submit a report
shell.settings.network.feedback.endpoint: Where reports are submitted. Defaults from your build configuration; admins can override here.
shell.settings.network.feedback.installa: Anonymous per-install correlation id. Share with support if asked.
shell.settings.network.feedback.endpoint: Endpoint must be an http:// or https:// URL.
shell.settings.network.crash.title: Crash reporter
shell.settings.network.crash.summary: Crash reports include the last 64 KiB of the console log and the stack trace, both with paths and emails redacted.
shell.settings.network.crash.pendingCoun: {count, plural, one {# crash report} other {# crash reports}} pending submission.
shell.settings.network.crash.localCount: {count, plural, one {# crash report} other {# crash reports}} captured locally before you opted in.
shell.settings.network.crash.clearConfir: Every queued crash report will be dropped without being submitted. This cannot be undone.
shell.feedback.title: Send feedback
shell.feedback.optInBanner: Feedback is off — enable it in Settings → Privacy → Send feedback before reporting.
shell.feedback.endpointMissingBanner: No feedback endpoint is configured. Ask your admin or set one in Settings → Privacy.
shell.feedback.kind.question: Question
shell.feedback.emailHint: Optional. Only sent if you fill it in.
shell.feedback.includeLog: Include recent log excerpt (vault paths + emails + credentials redacted)
shell.feedback.errorUnknown: Something went wrong. Try again later.
